---
title: "Local Integration Testing"
subtitle: "R unit tests, pytest, fixtures, and integration test collection"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

The test suite is organized around **integration tests as the primary evaluation
metric**. Integration tests run against real pipeline output and catch bugs that
static fixtures cannot â€” GCTA marker exclusions, Hive partition path construction,
DuckDB view registration, Nextflow channel wiring, and schema drift between what
R writes and what downstream queries read. Unit tests support them by pinning
pure-computation correctness (hash determinism, ANOVA golden values, threshold
math) where no real pipeline output is needed or helpful.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '16px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef primary fill:#48b96e,stroke:#2d8150,color:#fff
    classDef supporting fill:#7ab0d4,stroke:#4a80a4,color:#fff
    classDef gate fill:#e07b3a,stroke:#a3572a,color:#fff
    classDef data fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    A([Rscript tests/run_tests.R]):::data
    B{TEST_DB_DIR set?}:::gate
    C[Unit tests â€” spec and regression guards]:::supporting
    D[Integration tests â€” primary evaluation]:::primary
    E[pytest tests/pytest/]:::supporting
    F([Results reported]):::data

    A --> B
    B -- No --> C --> F
    B -- Yes --> C --> F
    B -- Yes --> D --> F
    A --> E --> F
```


## Test Tiers

| Tier | Files | Role | Requirements | Skips when |
|------|-------|------|-------------|------------|
| **Integration** | `test-db_structure.R`, `test-cross_validation.R`, `test-assessment_cross_validation.R` | **Primary** â€” evaluate pipeline correctness with real output | `TEST_DB_DIR`, `TEST_WORK_DIR`, `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT`, `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT` | Env vars unset |
| Unit | all other `test-*.R` | Supporting â€” pin pure-computation correctness | Static fixtures only; no pipeline run needed | â€” |
| Python unit | `tests/pytest/test_create_causal_vars.py` | Supporting â€” validate Python scripts | `pytest`, `numpy`, `pandas` in Python env | `pytest` not installed |

Run all tiers with a single command; integration tests skip automatically when
their environment variables are absent:

```bash
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```


## Integration Tests

Integration tests are the primary evaluation metric for pipeline correctness.
They run against real pipeline output produced by `tests/collect_test_data.sh`
and are the authoritative check that the pipeline is working correctly end-to-end.
Unit tests check isolated R functions with static fixtures; integration tests catch
the broader class of bugs that only appear with real GCTA output â€” edge cases in
file format parsing, Hive partition path construction, DuckDB view registration,
Nextflow channel wiring, and schema drift between what R writes and what queries read.

### `test-db_structure.R` (27 tests)

Verifies the Parquet database written by `DB_MIGRATION_*` modules. Requires `TEST_DB_DIR`.

| Test group | What it checks |
|-----------|---------------|
| Directory structure | `markers/`, `mappings/`, `traits/`, `traits/causal_variants/`, `traits/phenotypes/` all exist |
| Marker set files | Parquet files exist; filenames derived from metadata hash lookup |
| Hive partitions | `population=` / `mapping_id=` directories exist with `data.parquet` files |
| Metadata files | `mappings_metadata.parquet` and `marker_set_metadata.parquet` present |
| Marker set metadata | Required columns; `strainfile_hash` is 64-char SHA-256; `strain_list` non-empty |
| Marker set schema | Correct 6-column schema; FK columns absent from data files |
| Mappings metadata | Core columns present; algorithm values `"inbred"`/`"loco"`; both PCA flags present; mapping IDs are 20-char hex |
| Genotype matrix schema | Long-format schema: `marker_set_id utf8`, `CHROM utf8`, `POS int32`, `strain utf8`, `allele float64` |
| Trait metadata schema | 13 columns including `nqtl int32`, `sim_seed int32`, `h2 float64`; `trait_id utf8` |
| Phenotype schema | `strain`/`phenotype` columns; no NA phenotype values |
| Causal variants schema | 6 columns; `QTL` values in `CHROM:POS` format |
| **Inbred marker invariant** | `meta$n_markers == marker_set_metadata$n_markers` â€” GCTA processes all markers for inbred |
| **Loco marker invariant** | `meta$n_markers < marker_set_metadata$n_markers` â€” GCTA silently excludes near-singular markers from loco output (issue111 regression guard) |
| Database queryability | `open_mapping_db()`, DuckDB table listing, `COUNT(*)` query, `db_stats()` |
| Mapping data integrity | All partitions non-empty; P âˆˆ (0, 1]; no NULL P; `var.exp` absent or all-NA |
| Query functions | `query_mapping_data()` returns joined data with CHROM/POS; `get_threshold_params()` returns valid BF and EIGEN thresholds |

### `test-cross_validation.R` (8 tests)

Compares DB Parquet contents against raw GWA files in the Nextflow work directory.
Requires `TEST_DB_DIR` and `TEST_WORK_DIR`.

| Test | What it checks |
|------|---------------|
| P and BETA round-trip | DB values match raw `.fastGWA`/`.mlma` files to 1e-12 tolerance |
| SE round-trip | DB SE values match raw source |
| AF1 round-trip | DB AF1 values match raw source |
| EIGEN threshold | DB `n_independent_tests` matches the pipeline EIGEN file |
| GWA column normalization | `read_raw_gwa_file()` returns 9 normalized columns (`marker`, `CHROM`, `POS`, `A1`, `A2`, `AF1`, `BETA`, `SE`, `P`) for both `.fastGWA` and `.mlma` formats |
| Full coverage | `query_by_mapping_id()` returns a non-empty result for every `mapping_id` in metadata |
| Marker count consistency | `meta$n_markers` matches actual partition row counts |
| **Loco/inbred invariant** | `loco$n_markers < inbred$n_markers` for the same marker set â€” regression guard documenting the GCTA exclusion behavior (issue111) |

### `test-assessment_cross_validation.R` (10 tests)

Compares `db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (DB path) against
`simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (legacy path). Requires `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT`
and `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT`.

| Test | What it checks |
|------|---------------|
| File existence | Both assessment TSVs present and non-empty |
| Hash determinism | `generate_mapping_id()` produces identical 20-char hash across two independent calls |
| **Column completeness** | DB assessment has exactly 35 columns matching the `format_assessment_tsv()` schema |
| **`calculate_simrep_performance()`** | Runs without error on real data; Power and FDR are in [0, 1] |
| **`designate_qtl()` categories** | Only the 4 valid designations (`Detected.CV`, `Missed.CV`, `CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval`, `False.Discovery`) appear in real data |
| Detection concordance | Detected flag agreement between DB and legacy paths is 1.0 for all mappings |
| Designation concordance | `designate_qtl()` category agreement is 1.0 for all mappings |
| Interval concordance | Jaccard overlap of `[startPOS, endPOS]` intervals is 1.0 for all detected QTLs |
| VarExp populated | DB assessment has at least some non-NA `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` values |
| VarExp concordance | `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` matches between DB and legacy paths within 1e-6 |


### Collecting Integration Test Data

Integration tests require pipeline output collected by `tests/collect_test_data.sh`:

```bash
# Run pipeline (requires Docker) and collect outputs
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh

# Collect from a previous run (no pipeline re-run)
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --collect-only

# Remove previous results before running
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --clean

# Run integration tests with collected data
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

The default pipeline run inside `collect_test_data.sh` uses `-profile test,docker`,
so Docker must be available for a local integration test collection run. Integration
test files skip automatically when the required environment variables are not set.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Related pages
- To collect integration test data on the HPC: [HPC End-to-End Testing](testing-hpc.qmd)
- For cross-validation and output validation tests: [Output Validation](testing-output-validation.qmd)
:::
